metadata.title: The Rat Bounty
metadata.notes: Calling Bullshit (Vox Style), film four. Hanoi passages verified verbatim (sewers for the colonial quarter, plague, per-tail bounty, tailless rats, slice-and-release; 'Entrepreneurs imported rats from other cities, and even began raising rats in captivity'). Color law: navy = tails counter, crimson = rat population, terracotta annotation. ONE modern echo (class caps at 18–19 under the US News cutoff of 20 — verified). Outro pads A12.

beats.narration_text: Hanoi, around nineteen hundred. A modern sewer system, built for the colonial quarter — and underneath it, a thriving civilization of rats.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Hanoi, around nineteen hundred. A modern sewer system, built for the colonial quarter — and underneath it, a thriving civilization of rats.

shot.note: cold open — colonial-era street with a sewer grate, flat light, no people; shot.focus on the grate

beats.narration_text: This is the story of a government program that paid for dead rats — and bred living ones.
beats.tts_normalized_text: This is the story of a government program that paid for dead rats — and bred living ones.

card.title: THE RAT BOUNTY
card.sub: when the measure becomes the target

beats.narration_text: The sewers spread plague-carrying rats under the city, so the French administration hired rat catchers and posted a bounty: cash for every rat tail delivered.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The sewers spread plague-carrying rats under the city, so the French administration hired rat catchers and posted a bounty: cash for every rat tail delivered.

viz.note: ink sewer map under a street grid; bounty chip: cash per tail

beats.narration_text: It worked — spectacularly. Tails poured into the colonial offices by the thousands.
beats.tts_normalized_text: It worked — spectacularly. Tails poured into the colonial offices by the thousands.

viz.note: navy tail-tally counter climbing by thousands

beats.narration_text: Then people started noticing something strange in the sewers: rats with no tails, alive and well.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Then people started noticing something strange in the sewers: rats with no tails, alive and well.

shot.note: a tailless rat silhouette by a sewer grate, drawn-plate look, flat light

beats.narration_text: A dead rat doesn't need its tail — but a rat catcher does. Ask what the bounty actually paid for.
beats.tts_normalized_text: A dead rat doesn't need its tail — but a rat catcher does. Ask what the bounty actually paid for.

card.title: WHAT DID THE BOUNTY ACTUALLY PAY FOR?

beats.narration_text: The hunters had done the math: slice off the tail, collect the bounty, release the rat — and let it breed you next month's tails.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The hunters had done the math: slice off the tail, collect the bounty, release the rat — and let it breed you next month's tails.

viz.note: catch → snip → release → breed cycle, ink arrows; the loop closes on itself

beats.narration_text: Watch the two numbers: tails collected climbs and climbs — and the rat population climbs faster.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Watch the two numbers: tails collected climbs and climbs — and the rat population climbs faster.

viz.note: navy tails curve vs crimson rat-population curve diverging

beats.narration_text: The pattern has a name — Goodhart's Law: when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The pattern has a name — Goodhart's Law: when a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.

card.title: GOODHART'S LAW
card.sub: a measure made a target stops measuring

beats.narration_text: Others skipped the sewer entirely and simply farmed rats, raising them for the tails alone.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Others skipped the sewer entirely and simply farmed rats, raising them for the tails alone.

viz.note: rat-farm grid: captive squares multiplying; 'imported… raised in captivity' verified verbatim

beats.narration_text: It never stopped: universities cap classes at nineteen because rankings count small classes as under twenty. Different sewer, same rats.
beats.tts_normalized_text: It never stopped: universities cap classes at nineteen because rankings count small classes as under twenty. Different sewer, same rats.

viz.note: class-size histogram with a cliff at 19|20 — caps at 18–19 under the US News cutoff, verified

beats.narration_text: So whenever a number becomes a reward, expect the number to improve — and the thing it measured to get worse.
beats.tts_normalized_text: So whenever a number becomes a reward, expect the number to improve — and the thing it measured to get worse.

card.title: THE NUMBER WILL IMPROVE. THE THING WON'T.
